text:
## Summary
Adds an explicit `batchingTier` option to the TS SDK so customers can opt into the new workload-aware batching SLO tiers (fast / standard / cost_efficient) on a per-request basis. This matches the python SDK addition and the server-side rollout plan (initially opt-in via header).

## API surface
- New optional request option: `batchingTier?: BatchingTier`
- `BatchingTier` is a string union: `"fast" | "standard" | "cost_efficient"`
- When set, the SDK sends `x-redwood-batching-tier: <tier>`.
- If omitted, the SDK does not send the header and server-side defaults apply.

Example:
```ts
import { Redwood } from "@redwood-inference/sdk";

const client = new Redwood({ apiKey: process.env.REDWOOD_API_KEY! });

const res = await client.chat.completions.create({
 model: "redwood/llama-3.1-70b-instruct",
 messages: [{ role: "user", content: "Summarize this…" }],
 batchingTier: "stand...

title: Introduce delegated token channel and unified refresh for pull/push streams (ergonomic parity)
preview: Motivation: There has been persistent cross-SDK divergence in how streaming consumers handle credential refresh and retries, resulting in token rotation races, duplicated refresh attempts, and awkward ergonomics when migrating examples between TypeScript, Go, and Python. This PR introduces a delegated token channel ...
text: Motivation: There has been persistent cross-SDK divergence in how streaming consumers handle credential refresh and retries, resulting in token rotation races, duplicated refresh attempts, and awkward ergonomics when migrating examples between TypeScript, Go, and Python. This PR introduces a delegated token channel abstraction in the TypeScript SDK that matches the semantics we iterated on in Go/Python: single-flight refresh, lease-window proactive refresh, and explicit channel handoff for pull and push style streams. Summary of changes: 1) src/auth/delegated_channel.ts: new DelegatedTokenChannel class that accepts a user-provided async refresh handler and exposes a lightweight getToken()/invalidate() API optimized for streaming hot-paths. 2) src/stream/pullstream_adapter.ts and src/stream/pushstream_adapter.ts: adapters updated to consume DelegatedTokenChannel; removed inline refresh...

title: feat(usage): add billed token + experiment cohort fields to Usage API client
preview: Context: Finance/RevOps + Product are analyzing Q1 pricing/packaging experiments and hardening metering. The backend Usage API now returns additional dimensions needed for (a) fair customer explanations (billed vs raw tokens, cache attribution) and (b) experiment analysis (cohort/variant attribution). This PR update...
text: Context: Finance/RevOps + Product are analyzing Q1 pricing/packaging experiments and hardening metering. The backend Usage API now returns additional dimensions needed for (a) fair customer explanations (billed vs raw tokens, cache attribution) and (b) experiment analysis (cohort/variant attribution). This PR updates the TypeScript SDK to expose those fields in a typed, backwards-compatible way. Changes: (1) Update Usage API response types to include raw token fields and billed token fields (rawPromptTokens/rawCompletionTokens/rawTokensTotal + billedPromptTokens/billedCompletionTokens/billedTokensTotal) and cache attribution (cacheHitTokens when returned by API). (2) Add experiment attribution fields to usage line items: experimentKey, experimentVariant, experimentCohort. These map 1:1 to the API payload when present; when the account/day wasn’t part of an experiment or the server doe...

title: Add request ID capture + logging helper for faster Support escalations
preview:
## Summary
Support escalations frequently stall because customers don’t include request IDs, or because the SDK drops the `x-request-id` header on error paths. This PR adds a small, opt-in helper that (a) captures Redwood request IDs from responses and error responses, (b) exposes them on thrown SDK errors, and (c) ...
text:
## Summary
Support escalations frequently stall because customers don’t include request IDs, or because the SDK drops the `x-request-id` header on error paths. This PR adds a small, opt-in helper that (a) captures Redwood request IDs from responses and error responses, (b) exposes them on thrown SDK errors, and (c) provides a safe logging hook so customers can copy/paste request IDs into Support tickets.

This is intentionally not enabled by default to avoid surprising logging behavior in customer apps.

## Motivation
- Support intake + escalation playbook now standardizes on collecting request IDs early (see linked SUP tickets).
- Streaming disconnects/timeouts are hard to reproduce without request IDs.
- Customers often ask “where do I find the request ID?” and we want a canonical answer in the TS SDK.

## What changed
- Added `requestId` extraction utilities (from headers) and an o...

title: Retry-After parsing (delta-seconds + HTTP-date) + jittered exponential backoff
preview: Context: As part of the cross-language SDK parity initiative (retry/timeout standard), TS currently only parses Retry-After when it is a delta-seconds value. The gateway and some upstreams also emit Retry-After as an HTTP-date (e.g. "Wed, 21 Oct 2015 07:28:00 GMT"), which we should respect to avoid retrying too aggr...
text: Context: As part of the cross-language SDK parity initiative (retry/timeout standard), TS currently only parses Retry-After when it is a delta-seconds value. The gateway and some upstreams also emit Retry-After as an HTTP-date (e.g. "Wed, 21 Oct 2015 07:28:00 GMT"), which we should respect to avoid retrying too aggressively during throttling. In addition, our retry middleware used a deterministic exponential backoff sequence, which can create synchronized retries (thundering herd) across many clients. This PR adds: (1) Retry-After parsing for both delta-seconds and HTTP-date formats, (2) jittered exponential backoff consistent with the parity standard, and (3) tests/fixtures to lock behavior. Behavior details: Retry-After parsing: If header is delta-seconds => wait = clamp(deltaSeconds*1000, 0..maxRetryAfterMs). If header is HTTP-date => wait = clamp(dateMs - nowMs, 0..maxRetryAfterMs...

title: Unify retry middleware and error shapes (request_id + retryable) with SDK standard
preview:
Context
We’re in the middle of the SDK parity initiative and TS currently differs from Python/Go in two customer-visible ways:
1) Retry classification: TS retry middleware has been overly permissive (observed retries on some terminal 4xx), which contributed to SUP-29438 (duplicate billing concern).
2) Error surfaces...
text:
Context
We’re in the middle of the SDK parity initiative and TS currently differs from Python/Go in two customer-visible ways:
1) Retry classification: TS retry middleware has been overly permissive (observed retries on some terminal 4xx), which contributed to SUP-29438 (duplicate billing concern).
2) Error surfaces: request_id is inconsistently propagated (INT-1182), and error types differ between streaming/non-streaming paths.

This PR refactors the retry middleware and error construction so TS matches the standard taxonomy and aligns retryable status codes. Retry-After parsing/backoff tuning will be handled in follow-up PRs (see PR-1307).

What changed
- Introduced a single error normalization layer used by both streaming + non-streaming requests:
 - All thrown errors extend RedwoodError and include:
 - requestId (from x-redwood-request-id/x-request-id header, with body fallback)
 ...

title: sdk(auth): align env var names + precedence with Python/Go (deprecate legacy names)
preview: Summary: Aligns TS SDK authentication environment variables and resolution precedence with the Python and Go SDKs as part of the SDK parity initiative. This change also introduces deprecation warnings for legacy env var names to reduce "works in TS but not in Python" issues and to standardize support/debugging instr...
text: Summary: Aligns TS SDK authentication environment variables and resolution precedence with the Python and Go SDKs as part of the SDK parity initiative. This change also introduces deprecation warnings for legacy env var names to reduce "works in TS but not in Python" issues and to standardize support/debugging instructions. Motivation: Support + SE feedback shows customers frequently set different env vars per language and are surprised by which value wins when both are present. We’re standardizing on a single set of env var names and a single precedence order across SDKs, with security-reviewed guidance to avoid leaking secrets in logs/errors. New behavior (matches Python/Go): Precedence order: (1) explicit client option (apiKey / bearerToken) (2) environment variables (standard names) (3) none -> throw a typed auth error. Standard env vars supported: REDWOOD_API_KEY for API key auth...

title: Expose typed overload error classes + Retry-After handling docs
preview: Context: As part of the Hosted circuit breakers + load shedding rollout, the API is returning stable overload semantics (HTTP 429/503 with a JSON error payload + correlation IDs + Retry-After guidance). In the TS SDK we currently throw a generic ApiError, which forces customers to string-match messages and often lea...
text:
Context: As part of the Hosted circuit breakers + load shedding rollout, the API is returning stable overload semantics (HTTP 429/503 with a JSON error payload + correlation IDs + Retry-After guidance). In the TS SDK we currently throw a generic ApiError, which forces customers to string-match messages and often leads to unsafe retry loops.

This PR adds typed overload error classes and doc snippets so client code can (a) branch on stable error codes and (b) implement bounded retries with jitter that respects Retry-After.

What changed: 1) New error types in the SDK: - OverloadError (base) extends ApiError and exposes: status, code, message, correlationId, requestId, retryAfterSeconds (parsed from header), and rawPayload (best-effort). - RateLimitedError: thrown for HTTP 429 when error code indicates throttling / quota / concurrency cap. - ServiceOverloadedError: thrown for HTTP 503 w...
links: Linear: ENG-4832 (SDK/docs update for retry guidance)
Linear: ENG-4822 (Define overload error codes and payloads)

title: Compact connection throttle + async iterable sugar; cross-SDK retry presets and version bump
preview: Context: Customer workloads and internal benchmarks showed bursty micro-batch patterns causing head-of-line connection contention and surprising ordering latency on long-lived streaming consumers. This PR introduces a compact connection throttle (lightweight per-host connection window + soft drop) in the TypeScript ...
text:
Context: Customer workloads and internal benchmarks showed bursty micro-batch patterns causing head-of-line connection contention and surprising ordering latency on long-lived streaming consumers. This PR introduces a compact connection throttle (lightweight per-host connection window + soft drop) in the TypeScript SDK, a set of ergonomic async-iterable helpers for streaming consumers, and a curated set of retry/backoff presets to align behavior across TS/Python/Go. The changes are intentionally additive and backwards-compatible for common call patterns; an opt-in config surface is provided for teams wanting deterministic throttle windows and pre-warmed connectors.

Motivation: Reduce tail latency spikes during high-concurrency streaming usage, simplify stream consumer code across codebases, and provide consistent retry semantics across SDKs so server-side rollouts and client-side fal...

title: introduce-compact-auth-orchestrator-and-multi-sdk-stream-ergonomics
preview: Motivation: multiple SDKs had divergent credential-warmup and stream-consumer ergonomics which leaked complexity into application code. This PR introduces a compact auth orchestrator interface and runtime shim that 1) centralizes credential warmup/prefetch logic, 2) exposes a minimal async pull-stream ergonomic faca...
text: Motivation: multiple SDKs had divergent credential-warmup and stream-consumer ergonomics which leaked complexity into application code. This PR introduces a compact auth orchestrator interface and runtime shim that 1) centralizes credential warmup/prefetch logic, 2) exposes a minimal async pull-stream ergonomic facade across TypeScript/ Python/ Go and 3) ships a set of opinionated retry presets tuned for low-latency streaming use-cases. The change is intended to reduce duplicate logic in application-level code and improve parity across SDKs. Summary of changes: -- Introduce compact auth orchestrator (ts): src/auth/orchestrator.ts, with a light-weight in-memory cache, lease hints, and prefetch hooks for token providers. The orchestrator exposes a small surface used by streaming connectors and session facades. -- Add pullstream ergonomic facade (ts): src/stream/pullstream.ts and public ...

title: SDK: add experimentStickyKey option + expose assigned variant on responses
preview: Context: Route-level experiments require deterministic assignment. The platform supports a stickiness key (preferred: stable per end-user or per tenant) that the router uses when bucketing a request into a variant. This PR adds an optional experimentStickyKey field to the TS SDK request options and includes small he...
text: Context: Route-level experiments require deterministic assignment. The platform supports a stickiness key (preferred: stable per end-user or per tenant) that the router uses when bucketing a request into a variant. This PR adds an optional experimentStickyKey field to the TS SDK request options and includes small helpers to read the assigned {experiment_id, variant_id} from response headers so customers can log/debug assignments. Summary of changes: 1) New request option: experimentStickyKey?: string on chat/completions/embeddings/rerank request option types. When present, the SDK sends header X-Redwood-Experiment-Sticky-Key (exact casing handled by fetch). 2) New response metadata: the SDK now captures experiment assignment headers (x-redwood-experiment-id, x-redwood-experiment-variant, x-redwood-experiment-config-version) and exposes them via response.experimental?.assignment for no...

title: Fix header merge for streaming in Edge runtime (preserve Authorization)
preview: Context: SUP-4884 (customer reports 403 when streaming from Next.js Edge). Root cause: in the streaming helper we constructed RequestInit twice and used object spread on headers; in Edge, Headers behaves differently and Authorization was dropped during the second merge. Changes: use a single canonical Headers instan...
text: Context: SUP-4884 (customer reports 403 when streaming from Next.js Edge). Root cause: in the streaming helper we constructed RequestInit twice and used object spread on headers; in Edge, Headers behaves differently and Authorization was dropped during the second merge. Changes: use a single canonical Headers instance; ensure Authorization is always set when apiKey is configured; add integration test using the edge fetch polyfill; update docs with edge runtime note. Release: v1.4.1.
